meta.title: CCA-F-2: Agents, Tooling and Reliable Context

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **use an agentic system only when a task genuinely needs tools or multi-step control flow**?

options.text: Split all prompts into three agents regardless of complexity
Require web search for every prompt even when the answer is stable
Always use a multi-agent workflow for every user message
Prefer a direct model response for simple factual requests

questions.explanation: **D** is correct because agentic systems add latency and complexity, so simple tasks are better handled directly. **A** is incorrect because arbitrary decomposition increases cost without benefit **B** is incorrect because unnecessary tool use slows the system and adds failure modes **C** is incorrect because agent overhead is not justified for simple prompts

questions.stem: A team is planning a solution and needs to **use an agentic system only when a task genuinely needs tools or multi-step control flow**. Which service or design choice should they make?

options.text: Prefer a direct model response for simple factual requests
Always use a multi-agent workflow for every user message
Require web search for every prompt even when the answer is stable
Split all prompts into three agents regardless of complexity

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because agentic systems add latency and complexity, so simple tasks are better handled directly. **B** is incorrect because agent overhead is not justified for simple prompts **C** is incorrect because unnecessary tool use slows the system and adds failure modes **D** is incorrect because arbitrary decomposition increases cost without benefit

options.text: Prefer a direct model response for simple factual requests

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because agentic systems add latency and complexity, so simple tasks are better handled directly. **B** is incorrect because unnecessary tool use slows the system and adds failure modes **C** is incorrect because agent overhead is not justified for simple prompts **D** is incorrect because arbitrary decomposition increases cost without benefit

questions.stem: A deployment is underperforming because the wrong approach was chosen. If the real requirement is **use an agentic system only when a task genuinely needs tools or multi-step control flow**, which correction is BEST?

options.text: Split all prompts into three agents regardless of complexity
Prefer a direct model response for simple factual requests
Always use a multi-agent workflow for every user message
Require web search for every prompt even when the answer is stable

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because agentic systems add latency and complexity, so simple tasks are better handled directly. **A** is incorrect because arbitrary decomposition increases cost without benefit **C** is incorrect because agent overhead is not justified for simple prompts **D** is incorrect because unnecessary tool use slows the system and adds failure modes

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **coordinate independent specialist agents efficiently**?

options.text: Let the reviewer start before any inputs exist
Run independent subtasks in parallel and merge their outputs later
Have the orchestrator do every task itself
Force all specialists to run strictly in sequence

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because parallel execution is a major benefit when subtasks are independent. **A** is incorrect because dependent review work cannot start meaningfully before upstream results **C** is incorrect because that discards specialization and coordination benefits **D** is incorrect because serializing independent work wastes time

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **set durable role, policy, and output constraints that should outrank later user wording**?

options.text: Hide them only in a user message example
Place those constraints in the system prompt
Use temperature as the main policy control
Store them only in retrieved documents

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because system prompts are the correct top-level channel for durable behavior constraints. **A** is incorrect because examples are weaker than explicit top-level instructions **C** is incorrect because sampling settings do not define policy hierarchy **D** is incorrect because retrieved content is not a substitute for instruction hierarchy

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **help the model choose and call tools accurately**?

options.text: Register dozens of overlapping tools with vague descriptions
Provide clear tool names, descriptions, and structured input schemas
Return side effects directly from the model without application control
Replace tools with hidden chain-of-thought instructions

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because tool descriptions and schemas strongly influence whether the model selects the right tool and argument shape. **A** is incorrect because too many poorly described tools reduce selection reliability **C** is incorrect because the application should control execution of external actions **D** is incorrect because reasoning text does not replace executable tool contracts

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **connect external capabilities in a standard way across tools, resources, and prompts**?

options.text: Use the Model Context Protocol (MCP)
Pass secrets through normal chat messages to the model
Create an unrelated custom markdown format for every integration
Treat screenshots as the only integration mechanism

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because MCP standardizes how models interact with external tools, resources, and prompts. **B** is incorrect because secrets should not be handled as arbitrary chat content **C** is incorrect because one-off formats reduce interoperability **D** is incorrect because screenshots do not provide a standard machine-usable integration protocol
questions.reference: [What is the Model Context Protocol (MCP)? - Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/getting-started/intro)

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **keep long tasks within model limits while preserving the most important information**?

options.text: Delete the system prompt and keep only the chat history
Summarize or compact older context and retain high-value state
Append all intermediate work forever
Increase temperature to expand the context window

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because compaction preserves salient information while reducing token load. **A** is incorrect because that weakens instruction control and does not solve token growth **C** is incorrect because unbounded context eventually exceeds limits **D** is incorrect because temperature does not change context capacity

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **resume a long-running task across sessions without depending on the model to remember previous turns implicitly**?

options.text: Ask the user to re-explain the whole project every time
Rely on a higher temperature to regenerate the same hidden state
Assume the model will remember everything from prior sessions
Persist task state explicitly and restore it when work resumes

questions.explanation: **D** is correct because explicit state serialization and restoration is the reliable way to continue long-running work across sessions. **A** is incorrect because manual restatement is inefficient and error-prone **B** is incorrect because temperature makes outputs less deterministic, not more stateful **C** is incorrect because session continuity is not guaranteed unless state is preserved

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **keep destructive or irreversible actions under human oversight**?

options.text: Pause for explicit confirmation before executing the irreversible step
Log the action after it happens instead of asking first
Proceed automatically if the overall goal seems helpful
Retry until the model stops hesitating

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because human approval is the right safeguard when an unexpected irreversible action appears. **B** is incorrect because audit trails do not prevent accidental irreversible harm **C** is incorrect because helpfulness does not replace authorization for destructive actions **D** is incorrect because retries do not create permission

questions.stem: Which option is MOST appropriate for the following requirement: **configure repository-aware Claude Code usage with shared instructions**?

options.text: Disable every permission control permanently
Use documented Claude Code configuration and repo-level guidance such as CLAUDE.md
Store all workflow rules in commit messages
Depend only on ad hoc verbal reminders in each session

questions.explanation: **B** is correct because Claude Code supports structured configuration and project instructions for repeatable workflows. **A** is incorrect because blanket removal of controls is unsafe **C** is incorrect because commit history is not the primary runtime instruction surface **D** is incorrect because session-only reminders are less consistent and harder to audit

questions.stem: Which TWO statements are correct about **reduce bad tool calls in a production agent**? (Select TWO)

options.text: Limit the tool set to capabilities the agent truly needs
Give every tool the same generic description so the model treats them equally
Add large numbers of nearly duplicate tools
Write clear descriptions that distinguish similar tools

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because a smaller relevant tool set reduces confusion and unnecessary choices **D** is correct because descriptive metadata improves tool selection accuracy **B** is incorrect because generic descriptions remove the cues needed for correct selection **C** is incorrect because overlapping tools increase ambiguity and error rates

questions.stem: A team is reviewing design choices for **reduce bad tool calls in a production agent**. Which TWO choices are valid? (Select TWO)

options.text: Limit the tool set to capabilities the agent truly needs
Add large numbers of nearly duplicate tools
Give every tool the same generic description so the model treats them equally
Write clear descriptions that distinguish similar tools

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because a smaller relevant tool set reduces confusion and unnecessary choices **D** is correct because descriptive metadata improves tool selection accuracy **B** is incorrect because overlapping tools increase ambiguity and error rates **C** is incorrect because generic descriptions remove the cues needed for correct selection

options.text: Write clear descriptions that distinguish similar tools

questions.explanation: **A** is correct because descriptive metadata improves tool selection accuracy **B** is correct because a smaller relevant tool set reduces confusion and unnecessary choices **C** is incorrect because generic descriptions remove the cues needed for correct selection **D** is incorrect because overlapping tools increase ambiguity and error rates
